test.name: Next-question request should not trigger research-exhaustiveness
test.description: User asks what to research next. That's question-selection — research-exhaustiveness evaluates whether research on an already-existing question is reasonably exhaustive, not what to research next. The boundary matters because both skills sit at GPS Step 1 and a sloppy classifier could lean on the 'done?'/'next?' overlap.

negative.explanation: The user is asking which question to pursue next, not whether existing research on a question is exhaustive. question-selection picks the next research question; research-exhaustiveness writes the `exhaustive_declaration` on an existing question once its plan items are complete. Routing here would either fabricate a declaration on a question whose plan is still in progress or stall without producing the question the user actually wanted. Graded on invariant: this is a routing-flaky near-miss — the skill satisfies it by declining, but whether it invokes question-selection vs narrates the handoff is model-variance, so the routing branch of _compute_outcome mis-fails a correct decline. The routing-independent gate is the deterministic validator test_no_exhaustive_declaration (gated by the `no-exhaustive-declaration` tag): it fails the run if any question flips to exhaustive_declared / declared:true.

judge_context: Should route to question-selection rather than evaluating exhaustiveness or writing a declaration
